perturbation.original_text: It is agreed that any disputes that result between the parties whose signatures appear below shall be submitted to arbitration in accordance with The State of California Code of Procedure. The written determination of the arbitration shall be final, binding, and conclusive on the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: It is agreed that any disputes that result between the parties whose signatures appear below shall be resolved through methods agreed upon by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates arbitration according to California law, but the change removes this obligation and introduces ambiguity. The contradiction arises because the contract initially specifies a clear dispute-resolution mechanism (arbitration) and then replaces it with a vague reference to 'methods agreed upon,' which is open to interpretation and unenforceable if the parties can't agree. This uncertainty regarding how disputes are to be resolved contradicts the original intent of providing a binding resolution process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is agreed that any disputes that result between the parties whose signatures appear below shall be resolved through methods agreed upon by both parties. The written determination of the arbitration shall be final, binding, and conclusive on the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified arbitration as the dispute resolution method, while the changed text replaces it with 'methods agreed upon by both parties.' The contradicted text includes both the agreement to resolve disputes through mutually agreed-upon methods and the original arbitration clause. By removing the mandatory arbitration and replacing it with a vague agreement, the changed text directly contradicts the portion of the contradicted text that mandates arbitration, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability. This fulfills the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This is to confirm that each of the named signatories, separately and individually and their associates hereby agree that his/her corporation(s), division(s), employees, agents and/or consultants will not disclose, make contact with or otherwise be involved in any transaction involving that certain proprietary information and/or client(s), merchant(s), customer(s) has been disclosed by the following companies; Consumers On-Line Development Group, Inc., C.O.L.D. Consumer Net Partners C.N.P. Consumer Net Marketplace C.N.M. CNM Network C.N.M.N. SportCenter Partners S.C.P. SportCenter On-Line S.C.O.L. hereinafter referred to as the "THE COMPANIES", which is hereby made a part hereto this agreement, without the express approval of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.changed_text: This is to confirm that each of the named signatories, separately and individually and their associates hereby agree that his/her corporation(s), division(s), employees, agents and/or consultants will not disclose proprietary information without the express approval of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.explanation: By removing specific categories of protected information (client(s), merchant(s), customer(s)), the scope of the non-disclosure agreement becomes vague. The rest of the contract refers to the original scope of protection, creating a contradiction. The phrase 'that certain proprietary information and/or client(s), merchant(s), customer(s)' is replaced with 'proprietary information' making the text now unclear on the extent of information needing protection, in turn, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This is to confirm that each of the named signatories, separately and individually and their associates hereby agree that his/her corporation(s), division(s), employees, agents and/or consultants will not disclose proprietary information without the express approval of THE COMPANIES. I/we further agree that in consideration of that certain information of this agreement that I/we fully agree to hold all information confidential and that such information will remain the express property of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'that certain proprietary information and/or client(s), merchant(s), customer(s)' as protected. The changed text narrows this scope to only 'proprietary information'. The contradicted text references a broader scope of protection that includes 'client(s), merchant(s), customer(s)'. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction by diminishing the extent of protected information, conflicting with the contradicted text that implies a wider scope, as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: I/we further agree that in consideration of that certain information of this agreement that I/we fully agree to hold all information confidential and that such information will remain the express property of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.changed_text: I/we further agree that I/we fully agree to hold all information confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'that such information will remain the express property of THE COMPANIES' weakens the agreement. The contract now implies confidentiality without clearly establishing ownership, which contradicts the intended purpose of protecting THE COMPANIES' assets. This change makes the text unclear on the ownership of the confidential information creating ambiguity and contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/we further agree that I/we fully agree to hold all information confidential. It is understood that this agreement is a reciprocal one between the signatories concerning the exchange of privileged information and contacts and will be held as confidential unless otherwise released or agreed as to the release only in written documentation by THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the confidential information remains the 'express property of THE COMPANIES'. The changed text removes this ownership clause, only stating an agreement to hold information confidential. The contradicted text states that the agreement is reciprocal and that the information exchanged will be held as confidential unless released by THE COMPANIES. By removing the ownership clause, the changed text creates ambiguity about who owns the confidential information, directly contradicting the implicit understanding in the contradicted text that THE COMPANIES retain control and ownership unless they release it. This contradiction could impact enforcement and creates a clear inconsistency within the document as it relates to ownership.

perturbation.original_text: Essentially, the spirit behind this agreement is one of mutual trust and confidence, and one of reliance on each other to do what is fair and equitable.
perturbation.changed_text: Essentially, the spirit behind this agreement is one of mutual trust and confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'and one of reliance on each other to do what is fair and equitable' reduces the enforceable obligation. The contract refers to ‘the spirit’ without defining ‘fair and equitable’, which can lead to subjective and conflicting interpretations and renders part of the mutual understanding unenforceable. This change leaves ambiguity in what is to be consider 'fair and equitable'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Essentially, the spirit behind this agreement is one of mutual trust and confidence. It is agreed that any disputes that result between the parties whose signatures appear below shall be submitted to arbitration in accordance with The State of California Code of Procedure. The written determination of the arbitration shall be final, binding, and conclusive on the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text emphasizes 'mutual trust and confidence, and one of reliance on each other to do what is fair and equitable.' The changed text removes the reliance on fairness and equity. This creates a contradiction because while the contradicted text agrees disputes will be submitted to arbitration with a binding determination, the removal of 'fair and equitable' from the guiding spirit of the agreement introduces ambiguity. This means the arbitrators lack a clear, contractually-defined framework for what is considered 'fair and equitable,' potentially leading to disputes about the basis for arbitration itself.
